mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows during rate decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.0%, weak labor market conditions signal deteriorating consumer spending power and corporate earnings pressure, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 7.0%, or t-statistic for mean return is > 0.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and capital expenditure expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive MoM changes in RSAFS and HOUST yield negative 60-day forward returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during post-recession recovery phases.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during post-shock recovery periods show no statistical difference from baseline (p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.01 over 5-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls signal expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive payroll revisions are negative in > 50% of observations, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 230.0, accelerating inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces monetary tightening expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during CPIAUCSL > 230.0 periods are positive in > 65% of cases, or mean return t-statistic is > 0.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP growth above 2.0% and positive retail sales momentum indicate robust aggregate demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition yields negative 20-day returns in > 55% of instances, or the interaction effect fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by > 0.5% over single-proxy models.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during late-cycle expansions reflects capacity constraints and margin compression, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns during late-cycle expansions show positive mean or Rank IC > 0.03, contradicting the contraction signal.
